metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Stack / CUDA Module Availability
Technical Action Required: No (guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests CUDA 12.4/12.6 modules for package compatibility. CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not officially supported, and CUDA 12.8.1 is available in the Ampere test stack.

ACTION
1) Clarify that CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not currently supported.
2) Provide test stack commands for CUDA 12.8.1.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Yijiang,

We do not currently provide official CUDA 12.4 or 12.6 modules. We do, however, have a newer test software stack that includes CUDA 12.8.1.

Please run:

module purge
module load rhel8/slurm
module load rhel8/global
module use /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/spack-modules/test/ampere-2025-06-01/linux-rocky8-zen3
module load cuda/12.8.1/gcc/kdeps6ab

Please let us know if you run into any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not promise unsupported module versions.
- Distinguish test stack from officially supported stack.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Module Request [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting the addition of CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 modules to the cluster for improved compatibility with recent deep learning packages [1]. Similar requests have been made in the past for different versions of CUDA, and in some cases, users have been directed to load specific module stacks to access newer versions of CUDA [2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Investigate the possibility of adding CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 as modules to the cluster [1].
2. If feasible, install the requested CUDA versions alongside the existing cuda/12.1 module [1].
3. Provide the user with instructions on how to load the new modules, if installed [2][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Are there any specific dependencies or requirements for the requested CUDA versions [1]?
- Have the user's research dependencies been met with the existing CUDA version or alternative module stacks [2][3]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your request to add CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 modules to the cluster. We will investigate the possibility of installing these versions and provide you with an update soon. If you have any further information or specific requirements, please let us know.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any new module installations do not disrupt existing cluster functionality or user workflows [1][2].
- Verify that the requested CUDA versions are compatible with the cluster's current configuration and hardware [1][3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-99885
[2] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/tensorflow.html::chunk::0001
[3] : HPCSSUP-98682
metrics.user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
Could we add a few more recent CUDA modules to the cluster? At the moment I can load cuda/12.1, but many current DL packages (e.g., recent PyTorch/TensorFlow wheels) target CUDA 12.4 or 12.6. Having cuda/12.4 and cuda/12.6available would greatly improve compatibility and avoid building from source.
If feasible, installing these alongside the existing cuda/12.1 (plus matching cuDNN where applicable) would be ideal. I’m happy to test the new modules once they’re deployed.
Many thanks!
Best regards,
Yijiang Dong

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Request for CUDA versions

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear CSD3 Support Team,
I am writing to enquire whether it would be possible to install more recent versions of CUDA on the cluster. At present, the latest version available via module avail is 12.1; however, my research depends on features introduced in later releases (12.4, 12.6, 12.8).
Thank you very much for your support.
Kind regards,
Yuval

/usr/local/software/spack/spack-modules/a100-20210927/linux-centos8-zen2
#module use /usr/local/software/spack/spack-modules/a100-20210927/linux-centos8-zen3

# 2. Install latest Tensorflow in a python virtualenv
python -m venv tensorflow-ampere-env
source ./tensorflow-ampere-env/bin/activate
python -m pip install tensorflow
export CUDNN_PATH=/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/cudnn/8.9_cuda-12.1/lib64
export XLA_FLAGS=--xla_gpu_cuda_data_dir=$CUDA_PATH
# 3. Test your installation (be in a interative session
# via sintr or submit a job to a gpu compute node)
cat << 'EOF' > helloworld.py
#!/usr/bin/env python
import tensorflow as tf
mnist = tf.keras.datasets.mnist

(x_train, y_train),(x_test, y_test) = mnist.load_data()
x_train, x_test = x_train / 255.0, x_test / 255.0

model = tf.keras.models.Sequential([
          tf.keras.layers.Flatten(input_shape=(28, 28)),
          tf.keras.layers.Dense(512, activation=tf.nn.relu),
          tf.keras.layers.Dropout(0.2),
          tf.keras.layers.Dense(10, activation=tf.nn.softmax)
          ])
model.compile(optimizer='adam',
          loss='sparse_categorical_crossentropy',
          metrics=['accuracy'])

model.fit(x_train, y_train, epochs=5)
model.evaluate(x_test, y_test)
EOF
```

## Running Tensorflow[¶](#running-tensorflow "Permalink to this headline")

Tensorflow can be run with a sbatch script similar to:

```
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --account CHANGE_TO_YOUR_ACCOUNT-GPU
#! Update next line to the gpu cluster of your choice.
#SBATCH --partition ampere
#SBATCH -t 00:20:00
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:1
#SBATCH

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Cuda12.2

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,
I hope you are well.
I am trying to install and compile a package on csd3 which requires cuda12.2 and I am aware that the cluster only has up to cuda12.1. I am therefore wondering if it is by any chance possible to have newer versions of cuda as a module on the cluster?
Thank you!
Best,
Janice

--ntasks-per-node=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=1
#SBATCH -o tensorflow.out
#SBATCH -e tensorflow.err

module purge

module load rhel8/slurm rhel8/global
module load python/3.8.11/gcc-9.4.0-yb6rzr6
module load cuda/12.1 cudnn/8.9_cuda-12.1
# Access to optional ampere modules (uncomment if needed)
#module use /usr/local/software/spack/spack-modules/a100-20210927/linux-centos8-zen2
#module use /usr/local/software/spack/spack-modules/a100-20210927/linux-centos8-zen3

# Activate locally installed python virtual environment
source ./tensorflow-ampere-env/bin/activate
export CUDNN_PATH=/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/cudnn/8.9_cuda-12.1/lib64
export XLA_FLAGS=--xla_gpu_cuda_data_dir=$CUDA_PATH

# run script (change helloworld.py to your own)
python helloworld.py
```

## Running Tensorflow in containers[¶](#running-tensorflow-in-containers "Permalink to this headline")

CSD3 also supports running inside apptainer containers. A Tensorflow container can be downloaded from the Docker Hub and run with:

```
# The next command generates a > 3.8 GB file in the folder it is issued. Use with care.
apptainer pull docker://tensorflow/tensorflow:latest-gpu
apptainer exec --nv ./tensorflow_latest-gpu.sif python ./helloworld.py
```

retrieval_trace.text:
Rapids HBM[¶](#recommendations-for-running-on-sapphire-rapids-hbm "Permalink to this headline")

Since the cpu-r-hbm nodes are running Rocky 9, you will want to recompile
your applications if they are coming from a different operating system or a different OS version.

It is possible to log into a login node with compatible OS/software by logging into *login-sapphire.hpc.cam.ac.uk*,
which will land you on one of the *login-s-\** nodes.

Alternatively, request an interactive node using *sintr*:

```
sintr -t 1:0:0 -N1 -n38 -A YOURPROJECT-CPU -p ukaea-spr-hbm(-flat) -q INTR
```

to rebuild and re-optimise your applications to get the best from these specialist nodes. The `-q INTR` option used above requests a higher priority interactive jobs, but does not allow for more than 1h wall time. If you need a longer interactive session, you will need to remove the option `-q INTR` from your `sintr` command line and queue for longer.

The per-job wallclock time limits are 36 hours and 12 hours for SL1/2 and SL3
respectively.

The per-job, per-user cpu limits are now 4256 and 448 cpus for SL1/2
and SL3 respectively.

These limits should be regarded as provisional and may be revised.

### Default submission script for Sapphire Rapids[¶](#default-submission-script-for-sapphire-rapids "Permalink to this headline")

There is no default job submission template dedicated to Sapphire Rapid nodes, but you might be able to make one for yourself by tweaking an Icelake template.

You should find a symbolic link to an Icelake default job submission script modified for the icelake nodes in your
```

Rapids HBM[¶](#recommendations-for-running-on-sapphire-rapids-hbm "Permalink to this headline")

Since the cpu-r-hbm nodes are running Rocky 9, you will want to recompile
your applications if they are coming from a different operating system or a different OS version.

It is possible to log into a login node with compatible OS/software by logging into *login-sapphire.hpc.cam.ac.uk*,
which will land you on one of the *login-s-\** nodes.

Alternatively, request an interactive node using *sintr*:

```
sintr -t 1:0:0 -N1 -n38 -A YOURPROJECT-CPU -p ukaea-spr-hbm(-flat) -q INTR
```

to rebuild and re-optimise your applications to get the best from these specialist nodes. The `-q INTR` option used above requests a higher priority interactive jobs, but does not allow for more than 1h wall time. If you need a longer interactive session, you will need to remove the option `-q INTR` from your `sintr` command line and queue for longer.

The per-job wallclock time limits are 36 hours and 12 hours for SL1/2 and SL3
respectively.

The per-job, per-user cpu limits are now 4256 and 448 cpus for SL1/2
and SL3 respectively.

These limits should be regarded as provisional and may be revised.

### Default submission script for Sapphire Rapids[¶](#default-submission-script-for-sapphire-rapids "Permalink to this headline")

There is no default job submission template dedicated to Sapphire Rapid nodes, but you might be able to make one for yourself by tweaking an Icelake template.

You should find a symbolic link to an Icelake default job submission script modified for the icelake nodes in your

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with connecting to TurboVNC, specifically encountering a "bind [::1]:5909: Cannot assign requested address" error when attempting to set up a port forwarding tunnel [1][2]. This suggests a potential issue with the port forwarding configuration or the VNC server setup.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve the issue, the user should try the following steps:
1. Ensure that the VNC server is running on the correct display number and that the port forwarding command is correctly configured [2].
2. Verify that the port number used in the forwarding command matches the display number of the VNC server [3].
3. If the issue persists, try using a different port number or display number [1].

QUESTIONS TO ASSESS (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact command used to start the VNC server and the port forwarding command?
- Have you tried using a different port number or display number?
- Are there any other error messages or output that may be relevant to the issue?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To troubleshoot the TurboVNC connection issue, please try the following steps:
1. Ensure that the VNC server is running on the correct display number and that the port forwarding command is correctly configured.
2. Verify that the port number used in the forwarding command matches the display number of the VNC server.
If the issue persists, please provide the exact command used to start the VNC server and the port forwarding command, as well as any other relevant error messages or output.

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When setting up port forwarding, ensure that the port numbers used are correctly configured to avoid conflicts or security issues [1][2].

SSH tunnel[¶](#manually-creating-a-ssh-tunnel "Permalink to this headline")

Some alternative VNC clients also support automatic tunnelling - the role of the login node in these cases may be described as the *gateway* or *jump host*.

If automatic tunnelling is not supported by the VNC client, it becomes necessary to perform an additional step in order to manually forward local port 5900+N (where N is the VNC display number) to the same port number at the VNC server host (in the above example, assuming the display number is 1, this would be from local client port 5901 to localhost port 5901 on login-e-10). On the Cygwin or Windows command line (similarly on MacOSX, and indeed on Linux if you wish to avoid the *-via* option) this tunnel can be created by doing:

```
ssh -L 5901:localhost:5901 abc123@login-e-10.hpc.cam.ac.uk
```

(effectively this is what the Linux vncviewer *-via* option does internally). The equivalent step is also possible with PuTTY. Keeping the login session which this creates open, in the general case with display number N you would then connect your local TurboVNC client to localhost:N. The SSH tunnel ensures that the connection is actually made (securely) to the correct VNC session.

Once again, in the 3D desktop case, the *localhost* in the above should be replaced by the name of the graphical node running the desktop, and the choice of login node in the specification of the tunnel becomes irrelevant.

If connection is successful, an XFCE desktop session should then appear inside a window on your local machine.

## Using 3D

# Connecting to CSD3 via TurboVNC (3D Visualisation)[¶](#connecting-to-csd3-via-turbovnc-3d-visualisation "Permalink to this headline")

Important

The graphical visualisation nodes *gpu-e-1,…, gpu-e-7* retired on Monday 10th July 2023 and were replaced by new NVIDIA L40 nodes *gpu-r-1,…, gpu-r-10*.

## Starting a new VNC session[¶](#starting-a-new-vnc-session "Permalink to this headline")

The recommended form of remote desktop uses the freely downloadable software [TurboVNC](http://www.turbovnc.org/). This is because TurboVNC, which is centrally installed on CSD3, contains an improved codec for better transmission of rapidly varying 3D images. Hardware accelerated 3D (see below) will still work with other forms of VNC (or e.g. [X2Go](https://wiki.x2go.org/)), although in these cases the performance may be decreased depending on the codecs available for compressing and decompressing the desktop images.

Since 3D visualisation-capable graphics cards are only attached to a subset of nodes, in order to use 3D the TurboVNC server should be started on one of these nodes. Please see the [startgfx](#startgfx) section for instructions for launching and managing these specialised remote desktops, however the general descriptions of first-time password setting and the SSH-tunnel method of connecting to the remote desktop that follow remain applicable in the 3D case. You will probably wish to try creating a non-graphical remote desktop initially.

If you don’t plan to make use of 3D graphics, then you should launch a VNC session on an ordinary login node as follows.

To start a VNC

## Frequently asked questions (FAQ)[¶](#frequently-asked-questions-faq "Permalink to this headline")

* I am unable to login to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk and now I get “Connection refused”.

  > + Make sure you are using the same userid, password or public key as you would to login.hpc.cam.ac.uk. As with the login nodes, failing to authenticate three times in succession to multi will cause your address to be blocked for 20 minutes, so if this happens please wait this long before trying again.
* I am using MS Authenticator but the TOTP code doesn’t work.

  > + If you still have the QR code and secret key on your screen then delete the account in your Authenticator and recreate it, making sure that the account is of the **Other** type (not *Personal* or *Work*). Otherwise please contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again, and select account type **Other**.
* When performing ssh to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk the QR code is replaced either by garbage or is broken up by blank lines, making it unusable.

  > + Setting **export LANG=en\_GB.UTF-8** in Mac terminals before performing the ssh may stop this happening - however if this has already happened you will need to contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again.
* Connection sharing doesn’t work with Filezilla and I keep seeing TOTP requests during file transfers.

  > + Please prefer to use the RDS gateway *rds.uis.cam.ac.uk* for file transfers. This will request TOTP less frequently.

The screenshot show that you connected to the login node and forwarded port 5915, but turbovnc is running on display :4.
The easiest way to deal with this, is to open a second ssh session that forwards the port belonging to the turbovnc server you've started.
If the output from command 'vncserver' is "Desktop...started on display login-e-15:4" then you should connect as
```java
ssh -L 5904:localhost:5904 ap2153@login-e-15.hpc.cam.ac.uk
```
Then open your VNC client as localhost:5904
I.e. Use the port 59XX (where XX is the display number) twice in the SSH connection command and again in the VNC client, as well as using the same hostname given in the output e.g. login-e-15

# Connecting to CSD3 via TurboVNC (3D Visualisation)[¶](#connecting-to-csd3-via-turbovnc-3d-visualisation "Permalink to this headline")

Important

The graphical visualisation nodes *gpu-e-1,…, gpu-e-7* retired on Monday 10th July 2023 and were replaced by new NVIDIA L40 nodes *gpu-r-1,…, gpu-r-10*.

## Starting a new VNC session[¶](#starting-a-new-vnc-session "Permalink to this headline")

The recommended form of remote desktop uses the freely downloadable software [TurboVNC](http://www.turbovnc.org/). This is because TurboVNC, which is centrally installed on CSD3, contains an improved codec for better transmission of rapidly varying 3D images. Hardware accelerated 3D (see below) will still work with other forms of VNC (or e.g. [X2Go](https://wiki.x2go.org/)), although in these cases the performance may be decreased depending on the codecs available for compressing and decompressing the desktop images.

Since 3D visualisation-capable graphics cards are only attached to a subset of nodes, in order to use 3D the TurboVNC server should be started on one of these nodes. Please see the [startgfx](#startgfx) section for instructions for launching and managing these specialised remote desktops, however the general descriptions of first-time password setting and the SSH-tunnel method of connecting to the remote desktop that follow remain applicable in the 3D case. You will probably wish to try creating a non-graphical remote desktop initially.

If you don’t plan to make use of 3D graphics, then you should launch a VNC session on an ordinary login node as follows.

To start a VNC

## Frequently asked questions (FAQ)[¶](#frequently-asked-questions-faq "Permalink to this headline")

* I am unable to login to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk and now I get “Connection refused”.

  > + Make sure you are using the same userid, password or public key as you would to login.hpc.cam.ac.uk. As with the login nodes, failing to authenticate three times in succession to multi will cause your address to be blocked for 20 minutes, so if this happens please wait this long before trying again.
* I am using MS Authenticator but the TOTP code doesn’t work.

  > + If you still have the QR code and secret key on your screen then delete the account in your Authenticator and recreate it, making sure that the account is of the **Other** type (not *Personal* or *Work*). Otherwise please contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again, and select account type **Other**.
* When performing ssh to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk the QR code is replaced either by garbage or is broken up by blank lines, making it unusable.

  > + Setting **export LANG=en\_GB.UTF-8** in Mac terminals before performing the ssh may stop this happening - however if this has already happened you will need to contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again.
* Connection sharing doesn’t work with Filezilla and I keep seeing TOTP requests during file transfers.

  > + Please prefer to use the RDS gateway *rds.uis.cam.ac.uk* for file transfers. This will request TOTP less frequently.
* I don’t have a

User is running heavy `rsync` from login node and getting disconnected. Recommended approach is session persistence (screen/tmux) or GUI transfer workflow; follow RCS transfer best practices.

Hello Yaniv,

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Data Transfer

Portal Action Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is trying to rsync a large dataset from their rcs project (cc234) and is being kicked out of the login node, likely due to the heavy load of the rsync process [1][3]. The user is asking for a dedicated data transfer node or if they should use an interactive job.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
The user can run the rsync command directly on the login node without submitting a slurm job [3][5]. If the data transfer is slow, the user can use a bash script to run multiple rsync commands sequentially [5]. The user can also consider using other data transfer methods such as scp or SSHFS [4].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
You can run the rsync command directly on the login node without submitting a slurm job. If you need to transfer a large amount of data, you can use a bash script to run multiple rsync commands sequentially. Please let us know if you have any further questions or issues.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes, only on the login nodes [3][5].
- The user should not run heavy data transfer processes on the login nodes, but rather use a bash script or other data transfer methods [3][5].

headline")

Eg: data under /lustre/group,
/lustre/archive or any group-owned data under other locations.

This data will require being moved to an RDS (scratch), RCS
(archive) or RFS (resilient, backup) project on CSD3.

Group data should be purchased as a group, which would typically be
done by the PI for your research group. Additionally, given the
quantities of data involved, we can perform the data migration for
your group so that it is carried out as efficiently as possible.

Important

Please encourage your PI to get in touch with us at
support@hpc.cam.ac.uk to discuss how we can assist the setup and
migration of group data to CSD3.

We will be attempting to contact all known PIs to assist in this
process, but being proactive here can help ensure that we can make
this transition as seamless as possible for your group.

#### 3. Purchase any required storage projects on CSD3[¶](#purchase-any-required-storage-projects-on-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

Purchases can be made via our [storage portal](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk).
Projects greater than 100TB in size are eligible for a volume
pricing discount.

#### 4. Migrate individual files to CSD3[¶](#migrate-individual-files-to-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

For relatively modest quantities of data less than 1TB, you should be
able to migrate these yourself with `rsync`. An example command to
use would be,

```
user@wbic-gate-3:~$ rsync --verbose --info=progress2 --archive --no-o --no-g \
 --whole-file --update --inplace --rsh \
 "ssh -T -x -o Compression=no -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com" \
 ~/

# Data Transfer Guide[¶](#data-transfer-guide "Permalink to this headline")

This section covers the different ways that you can transfer data
on to and off CSD3.

## scp command[¶](#scp-command "Permalink to this headline")

The `scp` command creates a copy of a file, or if given the `-r`
flag a directory, on a remote machine. Below shows an example of the
command to transfer a single file to CSD3:

```
scp [options] source_file user@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk:[destination]
```

In the above example, the `[destination]` is optional, as when left
out scp will simply copy the source into the users home directory.

You can make use of `login-[cpu|icelake|gpu]` login nodes for scp.

## rsync command[¶](#rsync-command "Permalink to this headline")

The `rsync` command creates a copy of a file, or if given the `-r`
flag a directory, at the given destination, similar to scp above.
However, given the -a option rsync can also make exact copies (including
permissions), this is referred to as ‘mirroring’. In this case the
`rsync` command is executed with ssh to create the copy of a remote
machine. To transfer files to CSD3 the command should have the form:

```
rsync [options] -e ssh source user@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk:[destination]
```

In the above example, the `[destination]` is optional, as when left
out rsync will simply copy the source into the users home directory.

You can make use of `login-[cpu|icelake|gpu]` login nodes for scp.

## SSHFS[¶](#sshfs "Permalink to this headline")

To provide an transparent view of your files on CSD3 you can make use of SSHFS.

```
sshfs

transferred. Rsync also validates each actual transfer by comparing checksums.

On directories containing many files rsync can be slow (as it has to examine each file individually). A less sophisticated but faster way to transfer such things may be to pipe tar through ssh, although the final copy should probably be verified by explicitly computing and comparing checksums, or perhaps by using rsync -avc between the original and the copy (which will do the equivalent thing and automatically re-transfer any files which fail the comparison). For example, here is the same copy of /home/abc123/results on csd3 copied to from\_csd3/results on the local machine using this method:

```
cd from_csd3
ssh -e none -o cipher=arcfour abc123@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk 'cd /home/abc123 ; tar -cf - results' | tar -xvBf -
```

In the above the cd command is not actually necessary, but serves to illustrate how to navigate to a transfer directory in a different location to the /home directory.

## More on login nodes[¶](#more-on-login-nodes "Permalink to this headline")

A login node is in terms of hardware equivalent to a cluster node, but is connected to the external network, and does not run jobs itself. It is intended for:

* compiling code
* developing applications
* submitting applications to the cluster for execution
* monitoring running applications
* post-processing and managing data.

On CSD3 there are multiple login nodes, representing each node type present in the cluster, to improve reliability, share the interactive workload and provide suitable development platforms for each

this transition as seamless as possible for your group.

#### 3. Purchase any required storage projects on CSD3[¶](#purchase-any-required-storage-projects-on-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

Purchases can be made via our [storage portal](https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk).
Projects greater than 100TB in size are eligible for a volume
pricing discount.

#### 4. Migrate individual files to CSD3[¶](#migrate-individual-files-to-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

For relatively modest quantities of data less than 1TB, you should be
able to migrate these yourself with `rsync`. An example command to
use would be,

```
user@wbic-gate-3:~$ rsync --verbose --info=progress2 --archive --no-o --no-g \
 --whole-file --update --inplace --rsh \
 "ssh -T -x -o Compression=no -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com" \
 ~/

# Data Transfer Guide[¶](#data-transfer-guide "Permalink to this headline")

This section covers the different ways that you can transfer data
on to and off CSD3.

## scp command[¶](#scp-command "Permalink to this headline")

The `scp` command creates a copy of a file, or if given the `-r`
flag a directory, on a remote machine. Below shows an example of the
command to transfer a single file to CSD3:

```
scp [options] source_file user@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk:[destination]
```

In the above example, the `[destination]` is optional, as when left
out scp will simply copy the source into the users home directory.

You can make use of `login-[cpu|icelake|gpu]` login nodes for scp.

## rsync command[¶](#rsync-command "Permalink to this headline")

The `rsync` command creates a copy of a file, or if given the `-r`
flag a directory, at the given destination, similar to scp above.
However, given the -a option rsync can also make exact copies (including
permissions), this is referred to as ‘mirroring’. In this case the
`rsync` command is executed with ssh to create the copy of a remote
machine. To transfer files to CSD3 the command should have the form:

```
rsync [options] -e ssh source user@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk:[destination]
```

In the above example, the `[destination]` is optional, as when left
out rsync will simply copy the source into the users home directory.

You can make use of `login-[cpu|icelake|gpu]` login nodes for scp.

## SSHFS[¶](#sshfs "Permalink to this headline")

To provide an transparent view of your files on CSD3 you can make use of SSHFS.

```
sshfs

benchmark_annotation.summary: Gaia Arcus cloud ceph connection issues

condition.preset_description: Naive docs+tickets baseline with RRF reranking.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS / MDS connectivity (Arcus cloud)
Technical Action Required: Yes (service-side investigation/mitigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (Ceph/storage administrators + network)
Urgency: High (filesystem hangs across multiple instances)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports Ceph shares on Arcus cloud hanging across multiple instances, with client session state showing repeated MDS reconnecting behaviour and filesystem access hanging. Because the issue affects multiple shares and instances, this is more consistent with a service-side CephFS/MDS or network-path problem than a single-instance misconfiguration. Rebooting instances may temporarily clear symptoms but should not be treated as the primary fix.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Acknowledge and treat as a service incident
   - Confirm the issue affects multiple instances/shares.
   - Prioritise service-side checks over detailed user-side debugging.

2) Collect minimal high-signal diagnostics from the user
   Ask for:
   - example affected instance name(s)
   - approximate timestamps of failures
   - exact mount command(s) or error output if readily available

3) Service-side investigation (storage/admin)
   Check:
   - Ceph cluster health summary and recent warnings
   - CephFS filesystem status and active/standby MDS state
   - MDS daemon logs around the failure window
   - client session reconnect/eviction behaviour
   - any ongoing rebalancing or recovery that could affect metadata availability

4) Network path verification (admin)
   Verify routing/interface configuration and any recent changes affecting CephFS traffic.

5) Ask users to retry only after backend checks or remediation have been completed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the detailed report. Because the hangs are affecting multiple instances and shares, this looks more like a service-side CephFS issue than a single-instance configuration problem.

We’re investigating the backend now. In the meantime, could you please share:
- the names of one or two affected instances,
- the approximate timestamps of the most recent failures,
- and one example of the mount command or error output you are seeing?

Once we have checked the backend state, we can advise whether any instance-side action is still needed.

Cheers,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Treat multi-instance CephFS hangs as a service incident; avoid shifting burden to users.
- Avoid making claims about root cause unless supported by service-side evidence.
- Suggest disruptive workarounds (instance reboot) only with explicit user confirmation and after backend stability is restored.
- Do not request or expose sensitive infrastructure details in user-facing messages.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Ceph Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with Ceph shares on the Arcus cloud, with metadata servers switching to reconnecting, causing filesystem access to hang [1]. This issue has occurred before, and previous tickets suggest that failover of the MDS may resolve the issue [1][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify that the clients can route to the back-end Ceph cluster [1].
2. Check the MDS logging for any underlying issues [1][3].
3. Consider failover of the MDS as a potential solution [1][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the error messages you are seeing?
- Have you tried rebooting the instances or verifying the Ceph client versioning?
- Are there any other relevant logs or output that you can provide?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
We are aware of the issue with Ceph shares on the Arcus cloud and are investigating the cause. In the meantime, please try rebooting the instances and verifying the Ceph client versioning. If the issue persists, we may need to perform a failover of the MDS.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not attempt to manually modify the Ceph configuration without proper authorization and expertise [1][3].

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS / MDS connectivity (Arcus cloud)
Technical Action Required: Yes (service-side investigation/mitigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (Ceph/storage administrators + network)
Urgency: High (filesystem hangs across multiple instances)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports Ceph shares on Arcus cloud hanging across multiple instances, with client session state showing repeated MDS reconnecting behaviour and filesystem access hanging. Because the issue affects multiple shares and instances, this is more consistent with a service-side CephFS/MDS or network-path problem than a single-instance misconfiguration. Rebooting instances may temporarily clear symptoms but should not be treated as the primary fix.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Acknowledge and treat as a service incident
   - Confirm the issue affects multiple instances/shares.
   - Prioritise service-side checks over detailed user-side debugging.

2) Collect minimal high-signal diagnostics from the user
   Ask for:
   - example affected instance name(s)
   - approximate timestamps of failures
   - exact mount command(s) or error output if readily available

3) Service-side investigation (storage/admin)
   Check:
   - Ceph cluster health summary and recent warnings
   - CephFS filesystem status and active/standby MDS state
   - MDS daemon logs around the failure window
   - client session reconnect/eviction behaviour
   - any ongoing rebalancing or recovery that could affect metadata availability

4) Network path verification (admin)
   Verify routing/interface configuration and any recent changes affecting CephFS traffic.

5) Ask users to retry only after backend checks or remediation have been completed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the detailed report. Because the hangs are affecting multiple instances and shares, this looks more like a service-side CephFS issue than a single-instance configuration problem.

We’re investigating the backend now. In the meantime, could you please share:
- the names of one or two affected instances,
- the approximate timestamps of the most recent failures,
- and one example of the mount command or error output you are seeing?

Once we have checked the backend state, we can advise whether any instance-side action is still needed.

Cheers,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Treat multi-instance CephFS hangs as a service incident; avoid shifting burden to users.
- Avoid making claims about root cause unless supported by service-side evidence.
- Suggest disruptive workarounds (instance reboot) only with explicit user confirmation and after backend stability is restored.
- Do not request or expose sensitive infrastructure details in user-facing messages.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Ceph Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with Ceph shares on the Arcus cloud, with metadata servers switching to reconnecting, causing filesystem access to hang [1]. This issue has occurred before, and previous tickets suggest that failover of the MDS may resolve the issue [1][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify that the clients can route to the back-end Ceph cluster [1].
2. Check the MDS logging for any underlying issues [1][3].
3. Consider failover of the MDS as a potential solution [1][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the error messages you are seeing?
- Have you tried rebooting the instances or verifying the Ceph client versioning?
- Are there any other relevant logs or output that you can provide?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
We are aware of the issue with Ceph shares on the Arcus cloud and are investigating the cause. In the meantime, please try rebooting the instances and verifying the Ceph client versioning. If the issue persists, we may need to perform a failover of the MDS.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not attempt to manually modify the Ceph configuration without proper authorization and expertise [1][3].
- Ensure that all changes are properly documented and communicated to the relevant parties [1][3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-86663
[3] : HPCSSUP-87130

we cannot always retrieve deleted files (particularly short-lived files). Hence please be careful when deleting! If you need to retrieve a lost or damaged file, you should have the full pathname, and know, as closely as possible, when the file was damaged and when the file was created. Each directory has a hidden `.snapshot` subdirectory containing previous versions of data. Under these it is possible to search and browse for a good version of the file (which can be copied back to your home directory). For example,

```
/home/js123/workfiles/.snapshot/hourly_2022-07-06_18\:00/
```

contains an image of the /home/js123/workfiles directory as it was at 18:00 on 6th July 2022.

## Interconnect[¶](#interconnect "Permalink to this headline")

The CSD3 components are shown in the simplified topology diagram.
Only a few of the racks within the system, however it should be noted from a networking point of view each rack is composed of two 32 node leaves. Within this nodes are non blocking. To speed between racks the network is 2:1 in oversubscription. The GPU system differs in interconnect and uses Infiniband EDR. This is similarly 2:1 in its oversubscription, with 18 nodes per leaf switch. The GPU system accesses the Lustre storage via I/O nodes using LNET. These LNET routers do not show any performance reduction in benchmarks run both direct from the OPA attached compute nodes and the GPU system.

![../_images/OPA-Topo-sim.png](../_images/OPA-Topo-sim.png)

```
/home/js123/workfiles/.snapshot/hourly_2022-07-06_18\:00/
```

contains an image of the /home/js123/workfiles directory as it was at 18:00 on 6th July 2022.

## Interconnect[¶](#interconnect "Permalink to this headline")

The CSD3 components are shown in the simplified topology diagram.
Only a few of the racks within the system, however it should be noted from a networking point of view each rack is composed of two 32 node leaves. Within this nodes are non blocking. To speed between racks the network is 2:1 in oversubscription. The GPU system differs in interconnect and uses Infiniband EDR. This is similarly 2:1 in its oversubscription, with 18 nodes per leaf switch. The GPU system accesses the Lustre storage via I/O nodes using LNET. These LNET routers do not show any performance reduction in benchmarks run both direct from the OPA attached compute nodes and the GPU system.

![../_images/OPA-Topo-sim.png](../_images/OPA-Topo-sim.png)

## File IO Performance Guidelines[¶](#file-io-performance-guidelines "Permalink to this headline")

RDS is build from 5

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: CORE Procurement / Quote Clarification
Technical Action Required: No (quote preparation and clarification)
Finance Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting pricing for 10,000 additional GPU hours and additional RDS storage on `QAKG-SL2-GPU`. Because the message says `20 GB`, which is unusually small in this procurement context, the safest response is to confirm the intended storage amount and whether the quote should cover only new storage or also any existing storage renewal/extension before issuing a final figure.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Clarify the storage request before quoting
   - Confirm whether `20 GB` is correct or a typo.
   - Confirm whether the user means additional capacity on top of the current licensed storage.
   - Confirm whether the quote should cover only extra storage or also renewal/extension of existing storage.

2) Quote the GPU hours separately using the current applicable rate.

3) Once the storage details are confirmed, issue a written email quote covering both the GPU hours and the corrected storage amount.

4) Ask the user to send the PO to the ticket once ready.

5) Keep any later account-creation request separate from the pricing calculation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Wenyu,

Before I finalise the storage part of the quote, could you please confirm whether `20 GB` is correct? That is unusually small for this service, so I want to make sure we quote the right storage amount and whether this is additional capacity or an extension of your current storage.

Once you confirm the intended storage quantity and scope, I can provide the full written quote for both storage and GPU hours.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not silently convert user-supplied units.
- If the storage quantity looks implausible, ask before quoting.
- Keep quote assumptions explicit, especially if renewal or extension of existing storage affects cost.

Technical Action Required: Yes [3]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a quote for 10000 extra GPU hours and 20 GB extra RDS space for project 'QAKG-SL2-GPU' [3]. The cost of GPU hours is currently £0.55 per unit hour [3]. 

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To provide a quote, we need to calculate the cost of 10000 GPU hours, which is 10000 * £0.55 = £5500 [3]. For the RDS space, we need more information about the current pricing [4]. 

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the current price for 20 GB of RDS space?
- Is there a purchase order attached for the requested top-up?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
The cost of 10000 GPU hours is £5500. For the RDS space, we need more information about the current pricing. Please let us know the current price for 20 GB of RDS space.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- All costs are covered including capital depreciation of the machine, all running costs including power, management staff and support staff [6].
- The cost of GPU hours is currently £0.55 per unit hour [3].

```
This lists the charge for each job in the CompHrs column. Since this example queries usage of a -CPU project, these are CPU core hours. Similarly, for a -GPU project they would be GPU hours.

**I would like to add core hours to a particular member of my group**

```
gdeposit -z 10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu
```

This coordinator of the HALOS-SL2-GPU might use this to add 10000 GPU hours to the HALOS-SL2-SPQR1-GPU subproject assigned to the user spqr1. Note that if a compute hour limit applies to the parent of the project in the project hierarchy - i.e. if the parent project HALOS-SL2-GPU has an overall compute hour limit (which it almost certainly does) - then the global limit will still apply across all per-user projects.

Compute hours may be added to a project by a designated project coordinator user. Reducing the compute hours available to a project is also possible by adding a negative number of hours via the –time= syntax, e.g. the following command undoes the above:

```
gdeposit --time=-10000 -p halos-sl2-spqr1-gpu
```

## Submitting jobs[¶](#submitting-jobs "Permalink to this headline")

### Sample submission scripts[¶](#sample-submission-scripts "Permalink to this headline")

In normal use of SLURM, one creates a batch job which is a shell script containing the set of commands to run, plus the resource requirements for the job which are coded as specially formatted shell comments at the top of the script. The batch job script is then submitted to

## Service Level 3 – Free Usage[¶](#service-level-3-free-usage "Permalink to this headline")

Service Level 3 (SL3) operates with the medium quality of service, QOS2. QOS2 is lower than QOS1 which is used in SL1 & SL2. This service level is designed for groups with medium usage requirements who currently do not have funding to pay for their usage, thus an immediate conversion of funds into credits is not required.

SL3 is capped with a maximum number of usage credits per PI per quarter. This has been introduced to promote a more even usage of the free time on the system. Currently each PI may receive **200,000 CPU core hours** and **3000 GPU hours** per quarter.

There is no guaranteed minimum usage level for SL3 and there is no concept of expiry or of moving core hours across quarters.

Once a group in SL3 has consumed all its allowed core hours in a quarter, jobs can still be submitted at Service Level 4.

Service Level 4 (SL4) operates with the lowest quality of service, QOS3. QOS3 is the
```

User reports inability to SSH shortly after maintenance completion notice. Correct response is that services may still be coming online; direct users to service status and communications.

ACTION

2) Provide status page and advise monitoring updates.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Ieva,

We are still bringing services online following maintenance. Please keep an eye on service communications and the status page for updates:
https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/service-status

Best regards,

Technical Action Required: Possibly
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging in to the HPC via SSH, despite receiving an email confirming the end of maintenance work [1][5]. The user has tried logging in with their credentials and Microsoft 2-factor authenticator, but is still unable to access the HPC [1].

ACTION
Check the official HPC documentation for any updates on login issues or maintenance [2][4]. Verify that the user's account is active and that they have set up their MFA correctly [2][4]. If the issue persists, try logging in using a different method, such as the RDS gateway [4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the error message you are receiving when trying to log in?
- Have you tried resetting your MFA secret or recreating your account in the Authenticator app [4]?
- Are you using the correct login node (e.g. login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk) [2]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for reaching out. I've checked the official HPC documentation and verified that my account is active and my MFA is set up correctly. I'll try logging in using a different method, such as the RDS gateway. If the issue persists, please let me know and I'll provide more details about the error message I'm receiving.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always follow the official HPC documentation and guidelines for troubleshooting and resolving login issues [2][4][6].
- If you think you have shared sensitive information, make sure to reset the exposed credentials and let us know by contacting support@hpc.cam.ac.uk [6].

# Quick Start[¶](#quick-start "Permalink to this headline")

Important

* If you are a brand new internal user to the HPC service and have not submitted a HPC Account Application form, please visit <https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application>. Do not attempt to login without submitting the form.
* If you are a first time user OR have not used the system since **1st November 2022**, you will need to set up your MFA. Follow the [MFA guidance documentation](/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html#walkthrough-ssh-to-multi-hpc-cam-ac-uk)

## First Time Accessing CSD3[¶](#first-time-accessing-csd3 "Permalink to this headline")

All users of the HPC service will be required to have MFA set up in order to access and use the services.

It is strongly recommended that you read through the [MultiFactor Authentication (MFA)](/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html#walkthrough-ssh-to-multi-hpc-cam-ac-uk) user documentation to assist with the setup of your MFA.

For an introduction to CSD3, how it works, who can access and how, in video format, please visit <https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/getting-started-csd3-tutorials>

## Login[¶](#login "Permalink to this headline")

All users should contact CSD3 initially through the login nodes.

To access the CPU cluster nodes (for use with CPU compute nodes):

```
ssh <username>@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk
```

Research Computing Services use CRSids where they exist and so the <username>@ can usually be omitted if your University of Cambridge system does also. Internal Cambridge users should use their UIS password (at least initially). SSH keys can also be used (but *always* set a

* I am unable to login to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk and now I get “Connection refused”.

  > + Make sure you are using the same userid, password or public key as you would to login.hpc.cam.ac.uk. As with the login nodes, failing to authenticate three times in succession to multi will cause your address to be blocked for 20 minutes, so if this happens please wait this long before trying again.
* I am using MS Authenticator but the TOTP code doesn’t work.

  > + If you still have the QR code and secret key on your screen then delete the account in your Authenticator and recreate it, making sure that the account is of the **Other** type (not *Personal* or *Work*). Otherwise please contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again, and select account type **Other**.
* When performing ssh to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk the QR code is replaced either by garbage or is broken up by blank lines, making it unusable.

  > + Setting **export LANG=en\_GB.UTF-8** in Mac terminals before performing the ssh may stop this happening - however if this has already happened you will need to contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again.
* Connection sharing doesn’t work with Filezilla and I keep seeing TOTP requests during file transfers.

  > + Please prefer to use the RDS gateway *rds.uis.cam.ac.uk* for file transfers. This will request TOTP less frequently.

benchmark_annotation.summary: Request for ~/rfs Symlink in Home Directory

condition.preset_description: Naive docs+tickets baseline with RRF reranking.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Filesystem / Symlink / Home Quota
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a convenience symlink from `~/rfs` to `/rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw`. The correct command is straightforward, but the ticket shows the attempt failed because the user's `/home` directory was already over quota. In that case the symlink itself cannot be created until home usage is reduced.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to create the link with the concrete command:
   - `cd $HOME`
   - `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs`
2) If they see `No space left on device`, ask for `quota` output.
3) Explain that the immediate problem is `/home` usage above the 50 GB quota, not an RFS permission problem.
4) Advise them to remove unneeded files from `/home` or move suitable data to `hpc-work`.
5) Mention that accidentally deleted files from `/home` may be recoverable from `~/.snapshot`.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Hansheng,

Please try:

  cd $HOME
  ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs

If you receive `No space left on device`, please run `quota` and send us the output. That would usually indicate your `/home` directory is full, which would prevent creation of even a small symlink entry.

If your home usage is over quota, you will need to move some data into `hpc-work` or delete unwanted files from `/home` before retrying. If you remove anything from `/home` by mistake, you can usually restore it from `~/.snapshot`.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Use the concrete path from the user request when it is already known.
- Distinguish `/home` quota exhaustion from RFS access problems.
- Do not advise deleting RFS data to fix a `/home` quota issue.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Symlink Creation
Technical Action Required: No [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Low [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a symlink to be created in their home directory to point to their RFS project folder, which is accessible via the absolute path /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw [1]. According to previous tickets [1][5], users can create symlinks themselves using the command `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw rfs-iCNyzSAaucw` [1].

ACTION
Direct the user to create the symlink themselves by running the command `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw rfs` in their home directory [1][5].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
You can create a symlink to your RFS project folder by running the command `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw rfs` in your home directory [1][5]. If you encounter any issues, please let us know.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Users should create symlinks themselves whenever possible [1][5].
- Support staff should only create symlinks manually if the user is unable to do so themselves [5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-89742
[5] : HPCSSUP-89614

pricing discount.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Build/Compiler Toolchain / MPI Fortran Modules
Technical Action Required: Yes (workaround instruction)
Escalation Required: Optional (packaging improvement)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`gfortran 13.3` with Intel MPI hit `mpi.mod` read error (`Unexpected EOF`) because Intel MPI Fortran module files in that build were available only for older gfortran ABI versions. Workaround: explicitly include compatible `gfortran/11.1.0` (or `10.2.0`) MPI module path.

ACTION
1) Confirm exact module environment and minimal repro.
2) Explain ABI/module version mismatch.
3) Provide compile workaround with explicit include path.
4) Confirm successful compile with user.
5) Track packaging improvement for future stack.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Michael,

Please try compiling with an explicit MPI Fortran module include path:

mpif90 -I /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/gfortran/11.1.0 -c -DMPI precision.F90

(Using `.../gfortran/10.2.0` is also a valid workaround.)

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be explicit this is a compatibility workaround, not ideal long-term packaging.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Compilation / MPI
Technical Action Required: Yes
Portal Action Required: No

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with compiling a Fortran MPI code on CSD3, specifically with the `mpi.mod` file [1]. The error message indicates an "Unexpected EOF" when reading the `mpi.mod` file [1]. This issue may be related to the MPI implementation or the compiler version being used [2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve this issue, we recommend trying a different MPI implementation, such as OpenMPI, which can be loaded using the `module load openmpi/gcc/9.3/4.0.4` command [3]. Additionally, we suggest checking the compiler version and ensuring that it is compatible with the MPI implementation being used [2][6]. If the issue persists, please provide more details about the compilation command and the environment being used.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact compilation command and the environment being used?
- Have you tried using a different MPI implementation, such as OpenMPI?
- Are you using the latest version of the compiler and MPI implementation?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your prompt response. I will try using OpenMPI and check the compiler version. If the issue persists, I will provide more details about the compilation command and environment.
Best regards,
Michael

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When using MPI, it is essential to ensure that the compiler and MPI implementation are compatible [2][6].
- Mixing `.mods` between different Fortran compilers can cause issues [5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-10400
[2] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/modules.html::chunk::0004
[3] : HPCSSUP-82204
[5] : HPCSSUP-22110
[6] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/modules.html::chunk::0005

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Build/Compiler Toolchain / MPI Fortran Modules
Technical Action Required: Yes (workaround instruction)
Escalation Required: Optional (packaging improvement)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`gfortran 13.3` with Intel MPI hit `mpi.mod` read error (`Unexpected EOF`) because Intel MPI Fortran module files in that build were available only for older gfortran ABI versions. Workaround: explicitly include compatible `gfortran/11.1.0` (or `10.2.0`) MPI module path.

ACTION
1) Confirm exact module environment and minimal repro.
2) Explain ABI/module version mismatch.
3) Provide compile workaround with explicit include path.
4) Confirm successful compile with user.
5) Track packaging improvement for future stack.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Michael,

Please try compiling with an explicit MPI Fortran module include path:

mpif90 -I /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/gfortran/11.1.0 -c -DMPI precision.F90

(Using `.../gfortran/10.2.0` is also a valid workaround.)

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be explicit this is a compatibility workaround, not ideal long-term packaging.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Compilation / MPI
Technical Action Required: Yes
Portal Action Required: No

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with compiling a Fortran MPI code on CSD3, specifically with the `mpi.mod` file [1]. The error message indicates an "Unexpected EOF" when reading the `mpi.mod` file [1]. This issue may be related to the MPI implementation or the compiler version being used [2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve this issue, we recommend trying a different MPI implementation, such as OpenMPI, which can be loaded using the `module load openmpi/gcc/9.3/4.0.4` command [3]. Additionally, we suggest checking the compiler version and ensuring that it is compatible with the MPI implementation being used [2][6]. If the issue persists, please provide more details about the compilation command and the environment being used.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact compilation command and the environment being used?
- Have you tried using a different MPI implementation, such as OpenMPI?
- Are you using the latest version of the compiler and MPI implementation?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your prompt response. I will try using OpenMPI and check the compiler version. If the issue persists, I will provide more details about the compilation command and environment.
Best regards,
Michael

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When using MPI, it is essential to ensure that the compiler and MPI implementation are compatible [2][6].
- Mixing `.mods` between different Fortran compilers can cause issues [5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-10400
[2] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/modules.html::chunk::0004
[3] : HPCSSUP-82204
[5] : HPCSSUP-22110
[6] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/modules.html::chunk::0005

### GCC Compiler Suite[¶](#gcc-compiler-suite "Permalink to this headline")

The GCC compiler suite is accessed by loading the `gcc` module. For example:

```
module load gcc-7.2.0-gcc-4.8.5-pqn7o2k
```

Once you have loaded the module, the compilers are available as:

* `gfortran` - Fortran
* `gcc` - C
* `g++` - C++

### PGI Compiler Suite[¶](#pgi-compiler-suite "Permalink to this headline")

The Portland Group (PGI) compilers are available under the `pgi` modules. For example:

```
module load pgi/2018
```

Once you have loaded the module, the compilers are available as:

* `pgfortran` - Fortran
* `pgcc` - C
* `pg++` - C++

## Compiling MPI codes[¶](#compiling-mpi-codes "Permalink to this headline")

There are two prefered MPI libraries currently available on CSD3:

* Intel MPI
* OpenMPI

### Using Intel MPI[¶](#using-intel-mpi "Permalink to this headline")

To compile MPI code with Intel MPI, using any compiler, you must first load the
`intel/bundles/complib/2020.2` module (which on the cclake CPU nodes is
loaded by default as part of the `rhel7/default-ccl` module):

```
module load intel/bundles/complib/2020.2
```

This makes the compiler wrapper scripts available to you. To change the
underlying

## Compiling[¶](#compiling "Permalink to this headline")

Note that the `default-X` module, which is loaded by default on nodes of type X, arranges for `mpicc`, `mpif90` etc to be found and to use the recommended compilers and MPI implementation automatically when invoked. These wrapper commands supply the correct flags for compiling with the particular MPI implementation to which they belong.

When compiling code, it is usually possible to remove any direct MPI library references in your Makefile as mpicc & mpif90 will take care of these details. In the Makefile, simply set

```
CC=mpicc
```

etc, or define

```
export CC=mpicc
```

etc in the shell before compilation.

If some required libraries are missing, please let us know and we can try to install them centrally (as a module).

## Running[¶](#running "Permalink to this headline")

Please note that the following resource limits apply:

On CPU, SL1 and SL2 users are limited to 4256 cores in use at any one time and a maximum wallclock runtime of 36 hours per job. On GPU, SL1 and SL2 are limited to 64 GPUs in use at any one time and a maximum wallclock runtime of 36 hours per job.
SL3 users are similarly limited to 448 cores (CPU) and 32 GPUs (GPU), all with up to 12 hours per job.
For more information, please see this full description of [service levels (SLs)](/hpc/user-guide/policies.html#service-levels).

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Execution / MPI under Slurm
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance with documented launch pattern)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User attempted direct `mpirun` and saw MPI startup/initialisation errors. For this environment, MPI jobs should be run inside a Slurm allocation using the documented submission pattern. Running directly from a login shell is a common cause of PMPI/launcher errors.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm launch context
   - Ask whether command was run:
     - directly on a login node, or
     - inside a Slurm job allocation.

2) Provide documented MPI job pattern
   - Instruct user to submit via `sbatch` with account/partition/resources set.
   - Recommend launching tasks with `srun` inside the job script.

3) Provide minimal working template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

4) If user needs interactive testing
   - First allocate resources interactively (documented method), then run with `srun` in that allocation.

5) Reference documentation
   - Share the MPI jobs section for the relevant partition.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Milena,

This error usually occurs when MPI is launched outside a proper Slurm allocation.

Please run your MPI command through a Slurm job script, for example:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

Submit with:

`sbatch <script_name>.sh`

Please also refer to:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/a100.html?highlight=mpirun#jobs-requiring-mpi

Using the documented Slurm launcher pattern should resolve this startup issue.

Regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid ad-hoc launcher advice that bypasses Slurm resource allocation.
- Keep examples aligned with documented partition/account usage.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Job Execution [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with running MPI-enabled processes on nodes, encountering a fatal error in PMPI_Init_thread with an "Other MPI error" [1]. This error has been reported in other tickets, such as [1] and [3], where users have had similar issues with Intel MPI on icelake nodes. The recommended solution is to use the Intel MPI Library provided by the oneAPI toolkit [6] and set the I_MPI_OFFLOAD environment variable to 1 [6].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check the user's submission script and ensure they are loading the correct Intel MPI module [6].
2. Verify that the user has set the I_MPI_OFFLOAD environment variable to 1 in their submission script [6].
3. If the user is using a hybrid MPI-OpenMP application, ensure they have specified the number of threads per process using the cores-per-task parameter [6].
4. Recommend using the Intel oneAPI compilers [6] and provide guidance on compiling and running the code.

QUESTIONS TO ASSESS THE ISSUE FURTHER
- Can you provide the exact submission script and code you are trying to run?
- Have you tried running your code on a different node type, such as cclake or skylake?
- Are you using any specific MPI implementation or version?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To troubleshoot the issue with running MPI-enabled processes on nodes, can you please provide your submission script and code? Additionally, have you tried running your code on a different node type? We recommend using the Intel MPI Library provided by the oneAPI toolkit and setting the I_MPI_OFFLOAD environment variable to 1.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When troubleshooting MPI issues, it is essential to verify the user's submission script and environment variables [1][6].
- The Intel MPI Library provided by the oneAPI toolkit is the recommended MPI implementation for use on our clusters [6].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-61542
[3] : HPCSSUP-76564
[6] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc-jobs.html::chunk::0002

mps-wrapper.sh usage:
# > srun [srun args] mps-wrapper.sh [cmd] [cmd args]

# Set CUDA device
numa_nodes=\$(hwloc-calc --physical --intersect NUMAnode \$(hwloc-bind --get --taskset))
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=\$numa_nodes

# Wait for MPS to start
sleep 1

# Run the command
numactl --membind=\$numa_nodes "\$@"
result=\$?

exit \$result
EOF

# Start MPS Server before running mpirun
# Only this path is supported by MPS
export CUDA_MPS_PIPE_DIRECTORY=/tmp/nvidia-mps
export CUDA_MPS_LOG_DIRECTORY=/tmp/nvidia-log-\$(id -un)
# Launch MPS from a single rank per node
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 nvidia-cuda-mps-control -d

chmod +x ./mps-wrapper.sh
cmd="mpirun --npernode ${SLURM_NTASKS_PER_NODE} -np ${SLURM_NTASKS} --bind-to none ./mps-wrapper.sh $app -sf gpu -pk gpu 4 -i $infile"
echo $cmd
eval $cmd

# Shutdown MPS control daemon before finishing
echo quit | nvidia-cuda-mps-control
```

where we are using an input file `in.colloid` to run on the GPU system, making use of all 4 GPUs on 1 compute node, running 2 MPI tasks per GPU thanks to the [CUDA Multi-Process Service (MPS)](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/mps/index.html) wrapper script detailed inside. This wrapper script allows to run efficiently and concurently multiple MPI processes on a GPU by making use of the Hyper-Q capabilities on the latest NVIDIA cards. This allowing to optimise the GPU usage when each MPI process only use a fraction of the GPU memory available (80 GB on the Ampere cards). Without the MPS, the MPI processes will be run sequentially, wasting GPU time.

To use more or less GPUs the `-N` and `-n` options

per node, i.e.:

```
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:4
```

### Jobs requiring MPI[¶](#jobs-requiring-mpi "Permalink to this headline")

We currently recommend using the [Intel MPI Library](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/oneapi/mpi-library.html) provided by the oneAPI toolkit:

```
module av intel-oneapi-mpi
```

To use GPU Aware MPI and allow passing device buffers to MPI calls, set the `I_MPI_OFFLOAD`
environment variable to `1` in your submission script:

```
export I_MPI_OFFLOAD=1
```

If you are sure that your code only involves buffers of the same type (e.g.
only GPU buffers or only host buffers) in a single
MPI operation you can further optimize MPI communication between GPUs by setting

```
export I_MPI_OFFLOAD_SYMMETRIC=1
```

This will disable handling of MPI communication between GPU buffers and host buffers.

### Multithreading jobs[¶](#multithreading-jobs "Permalink to this headline")

If your code uses multithreading (e.g. host-based OpenMP), you will need to
specify the number of threads per process in your Slurm batch script using the
cores-per-task parameter. For example, to run a hybrid MPI-OpenMP application
using 24 processes and 4 threads per task:

```
#SBATCH -n 24 # or --ntasks
#SBATCH -c 4 # or --cores-per-task
```

If you do *not* specify the cores-per-task parameter Slurm will pin the
threads to the same core, reducing performance.

### Recommended Compilers[¶](#recommended-compilers "Permalink to this headline")

We recommend using the [Intel oneAPI](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/oneapi/overview.html)

mps-wrapper.sh usage:
# > srun [srun args] mps-wrapper.sh [cmd] [cmd args]

# Set CUDA device
numa_nodes=\$(hwloc-calc --physical --intersect NUMAnode \$(hwloc-bind --get --taskset))
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=\$numa_nodes

# Wait for MPS to start
sleep 1

# Run the command
numactl --membind=\$numa_nodes "\$@"
result=\$?

exit \$result
EOF

# Start MPS Server before running mpirun
# Only this path is supported by MPS
export CUDA_MPS_PIPE_DIRECTORY=/tmp/nvidia-mps
export CUDA_MPS_LOG_DIRECTORY=/tmp/nvidia-log-\$(id -un)
# Launch MPS from a single rank per node
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 nvidia-cuda-mps-control -d

chmod +x ./mps-wrapper.sh
cmd="mpirun --npernode ${SLURM_NTASKS_PER_NODE} -np ${SLURM_NTASKS} --bind-to none ./mps-wrapper.sh $app -sf gpu -pk gpu 4 -i $infile"
echo $cmd
eval $cmd

# Shutdown MPS control daemon before finishing
echo quit | nvidia-cuda-mps-control
```

where we are using an input file `in.colloid` to run on the GPU system, making use of all 4 GPUs on 1 compute node, running 2 MPI tasks per GPU thanks to the [CUDA Multi-Process Service (MPS)](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/mps/index.html) wrapper script detailed inside. This wrapper script allows to run efficiently and concurently multiple MPI processes on a GPU by making use of the Hyper-Q capabilities on the latest NVIDIA cards. This allowing to optimise the GPU usage when each MPI process only use a fraction of the GPU memory available (80 GB on the Ampere cards). Without the MPS, the MPI processes will be run sequentially, wasting GPU time.

To use more or less GPUs the `-N` and `-n` options

per node, i.e.:

```
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:4
```

### Jobs requiring MPI[¶](#jobs-requiring-mpi "Permalink to this headline")

We currently recommend using the [Intel MPI Library](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/oneapi/mpi-library.html) provided by the oneAPI toolkit:

```
module av intel-oneapi-mpi
```

To use GPU Aware MPI and allow passing device buffers to MPI calls, set the `I_MPI_OFFLOAD`
environment variable to `1` in your submission script:

```
export I_MPI_OFFLOAD=1
```

If you are sure that your code only involves buffers of the same type (e.g.
only GPU buffers or only host buffers) in a single
MPI operation you can further optimize MPI communication between GPUs by setting

```
export I_MPI_OFFLOAD_SYMMETRIC=1
```

This will disable handling of MPI communication between GPU buffers and host buffers.

### Multithreading jobs[¶](#multithreading-jobs "Permalink to this headline")

If your code uses multithreading (e.g. host-based OpenMP), you will need to
specify the number of threads per process in your Slurm batch script using the
cores-per-task parameter. For example, to run a hybrid MPI-OpenMP application
using 24 processes and 4 threads per task:

```
#SBATCH -n 24 # or --ntasks
#SBATCH -c 4 # or --cores-per-task
```

If you do *not* specify the cores-per-task parameter Slurm will pin the
threads to the same core, reducing performance.

### Recommended Compilers[¶](#recommended-compilers "Permalink to this headline")

We recommend using the [Intel oneAPI](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/tools/oneapi/overview.html)

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / RCS Connectivity / SSH-MFA
Technical Action Required: No (user onboarding guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that multiple people are unable to connect to `rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk` over SFTP/SSH, raising the possibility of either a broader service problem or account-specific access changes. The correct first response is to confirm whether there is an active service incident and, if not, gather examples from affected users to determine whether the problem is shared or user-specific.

ACTION
1) Check whether there is an active service issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.
2) Ask for example usernames, client/method used, and the exact error messages seen by affected users.
3) If the service is healthy, distinguish between users who cannot access RCS at all and users with account-specific denial errors.
4) For individual users, confirm SSH/storage MFA enrolment if relevant and ask them to raise their own tickets for account-specific checks.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

We’re first checking whether there is any broader issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.

If the service itself is healthy, we’ll need the affected usernames, the client or method being used, and the exact error messages so we can tell whether this is a shared problem or an account-specific one.

For users who can access the web portal but not SSH/SFTP services, we may also need to confirm that SSH/storage MFA has been set up separately.

If any individual user continues to have problems, please ask them to raise their own ticket so we can check their account details directly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not troubleshoot third-party user accounts through one reporter without user-specific consent/context.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Connection Issue [1][3]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][3]
Portal Action Required: No [1][3]
Urgency: Medium [1][3]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
There have been previous instances of connection issues with the RCS, including cases where users were unable to connect due to network unreachable errors [5] or connection closed by remote host errors [3]. The current issue may be related to a similar problem, but more information is needed to determine the cause.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
Investigate the current status of the RCS service and check for any ongoing maintenance or issues that may be causing the connection problems [1][3]. If the issue is related to a previous problem, review the resolution steps taken in similar cases, such as rebuilding machines or resetting MFA secrets [2][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the error message you are receiving when trying to connect to the RCS?
- Have you tried connecting to the RCS using a different method, such as the RDS gateway [2]?
- Are there any other users in your department who are experiencing similar issues?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,
Thank you for reporting the issue with connecting to the RCS. We are aware of previous instances of connection problems and are investigating the current status of the service. Please let us know if you have any further information about the error message you are receiving. We will work to resolve the issue as soon as possible.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- When troubleshooting connection issues, it is essential to follow official documentation and consult with the Service Desk team to ensure that any potential security risks are mitigated [4].
- Users should be cautious when sharing sensitive information and should reset exposed credentials immediately [4].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-29314
[2] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html::chunk::0008
[3] : HPCSSUP-89893
[4] : https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/getting_support.html::chunk::0002
[5] : HPCSSUP-91273

## Frequently asked questions (FAQ)[¶](#frequently-asked-questions-faq "Permalink to this headline")

* I am unable to login to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk and now I get “Connection refused”.

  > + Make sure you are using the same userid, password or public key as you would to login.hpc.cam.ac.uk. As with the login nodes, failing to authenticate three times in succession to multi will cause your address to be blocked for 20 minutes, so if this happens please wait this long before trying again.
* I am using MS Authenticator but the TOTP code doesn’t work.

  > + If you still have the QR code and secret key on your screen then delete the account in your Authenticator and recreate it, making sure that the account is of the **Other** type (not *Personal* or *Work*). Otherwise please contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again, and select account type **Other**.
* When performing ssh to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk the QR code is replaced either by garbage or is broken up by blank lines, making it unusable.

  > + Setting **export LANG=en\_GB.UTF-8** in Mac terminals before performing the ssh may stop this happening - however if this has already happened you will need to contact *support@hpc.cam.ac.uk* to request reset of the MFA secret before trying again.
* Connection sharing doesn’t work with Filezilla and I keep seeing TOTP requests during file transfers.

  > + Please prefer to use the RDS gateway *rds.uis.cam.ac.uk* for file transfers. This will request TOTP less frequently.
